tables_info.table_text:
ConditionSentence
Example 1
HC医生成功地为病人做手术使病人及时得到治疗
The patient got treatment in time after the doctor successfully finished the surgery.
LC医生成功地为病人做诊断使病人及时得到治疗
The patient got treatment in time after the doctors successfully finished the diagnoses.
SV医生成功地为病人做轮胎使病人及时得到治疗
The patient got treatment in time after the Doctors successfully finished the tires.
Example 2
HC由于伤病刘翔错过了机会让人们都十分惋惜
The people felt sorry for Liu Xiang as he missed the game due to disease.
LC由于伤病刘翔错过了机会让人们都十分惋惜
The people feel sorry for Liu Xiang as he missed the chance due to disease.
SV由于伤病刘翔错过了别人让人们都十分惋惜
The people feel sorry for Liu Xiang as he missed their judgment due to disease.

tables_info.after_context:
Note: Three types of sentences, high cloze (HC) sentences, low cloze (LC) sentences, and violation sentences (SV), were adopted to manipulate the difficulty levels of the sentence-level semantic unification in both the implicit and explicit language tasks.
doi:10.1371/journal.pone.0080214.t001

## Page 6

Table 2. Brain regions used in constructing the human brain functional networks in the present study.
tables_info.after_context: These regions are originally described in the Automated Anatomical Labeling (AAL) template by Tzourio-Mazoyer et al. (2002), and the abbreviations are listed according to Salvador et al. (2005) and Achard et al. (2006). The same 45 brain regions were extracted from the right and left hemispheres to provide 90 regions in total for each subject.
tables_info.full_context:
## Page 6

Table 2. Brain regions used in constructing the human brain functional networks in the present study.


[TABLE 2]


These regions are originally described in the Automated Anatomical Labeling (AAL) template by Tzourio-Mazoyer et al. (2002), and the abbreviations are listed according to Salvador et al. (2005) and Achard et al. (2006). The same 45 brain regions were extracted from the right and left hemispheres to provide 90 regions in total for each subject.

tables_info.table_text:
Network properties DefinitionsDescriptions
Global parametersCluster coefficient \( C_{p} = \frac{1}{N} \sum_{i \in G} \frac{E_{i}}{D_{\text{node}}(i) (D_{\text{node}}(i) - 1)/2} \)Given a network G(N, M) with N nodes and M edges, \( D_{i} \) is the degree of node i. \( E_{i} \) is the number of edges in \( G_{i} \) , the subgraph consisting of the neighbors of node i.
Characteristic path length \( L_{P} = \frac{1}{N(N-1)} \left( \sum_{j \in G} \frac{1}{L_{j}} \right) \)\( L_{ij} \) is the shortest path length between nodes i and j.
Global efficiency \( E_{global} = \frac{1}{N(N-1)} \sum_{j \in G} \frac{1}{L_{ij}} \)\( E_{global}(i) \) is the global efficiency of \( G_{i} \) .
Nodal parametersLocal efficiency \( E_{loc} = \frac{1}{N} \sum_{i \in G} E_{global}(i) \)\( e_{ij} \) is the (i, j)th element in the formerly obtained binarized correlation matrix.
Nodal parametersBetweenness centrality \( N_{bc}(i) = \sum_{j \in G} \frac{\delta_{jk}(i)}{\delta} \)\( \delta_{jk} \) is the number of shortest paths from node j to node k, and \( \delta_{jk}(i) \) is the number of shortest paths from node j to node k that pass through the node i within graph G.

header_row:
Network properties DefinitionsDescriptions
Global parametersCluster coefficient \( C_{p} = \frac{1}{N} \sum_{i \in G} \frac{E_{i}}{D_{\text{node}}(i) (D_{\text{node}}(i) - 1)/2} \)Given a network G(N, M) with N nodes and M edges, \( D_{i} \) is the degree of node i. \( E_{i} \) is the number of edges in \( G_{i} \) , the subgraph consisting of the neighbors of node i.
Characteristic path length \( L_{P} = \frac{1}{N(N-1)} \left( \sum_{j \in G} \frac{1}{L_{j}} \right) \)\( L_{ij} \) is the shortest path length between nodes i and j.
Global efficiency \( E_{global} = \frac{1}{N(N-1)} \sum_{j \in G} \frac{1}{L_{ij}} \)\( E_{global}(i) \) is the global efficiency of \( G_{i} \) .
Nodal parametersLocal efficiency \( E_{loc} = \frac{1}{N} \sum_{i \in G} E_{global}(i) \)\( e_{ij} \) is the (i, j)th element in the formerly obtained binarized correlation matrix.
Nodal parametersBetweenness centrality \( N_{bc}(i) = \sum_{j \in G} \frac{\delta_{jk}(i)}{\delta} \)\( \delta_{jk} \) is the number of shortest paths from node j to node k, and \( \delta_{jk}(i) \) is the number of shortest paths from node j to node k that pass through the node i within graph G.

header_row:
Network properties DefinitionsDescriptions
Global parametersCluster coefficient \( C_{p} = \frac{1}{N} \sum_{i \in G} \frac{E_{i}}{D_{\text{node}}(i) (D_{\text{node}}(i) - 1)/2} \)Given a network G(N, M) with N nodes and M edges, \( D_{i} \) is the degree of node i. \( E_{i} \) is the number of edges in \( G_{i} \) , the subgraph consisting of the neighbors of node i.
Characteristic path length \( L_{P} = \frac{1}{N(N-1)} \left( \sum_{j \in G} \frac{1}{L_{j}} \right) \)\( L_{ij} \) is the shortest path length between nodes i and j.
Global efficiency \( E_{global} = \frac{1}{N(N-1)} \sum_{j \in G} \frac{1}{L_{ij}} \)\( E_{global}(i) \) is the global efficiency of \( G_{i} \) .
Nodal parametersLocal efficiency \( E_{loc} = \frac{1}{N} \sum_{i \in G} E_{global}(i) \)\( e_{ij} \) is the (i, j)th element in the formerly obtained binarized correlation matrix.
Nodal parametersBetweenness centrality \( N_{bc}(i) = \sum_{j \in G} \frac{\delta_{jk}(i)}{\delta} \)\( \delta_{jk} \) is the number of shortest paths from node j to node k, and \( \delta_{jk}(i) \) is the number of shortest paths from node j to node k that pass through the node i within graph G.

Table 4. Integrated global parameters mean (SD) of the human brain functional networks and their statistical difference between the explicit and implicit language tasks.
tables_info.after_context: Note:  \( C_{p}^{int}, L_{p}^{int}, \gamma_{p}^{int}, \lambda_{p}^{int}, E_{glob}^{int} \) , and  \( E_{loc}^{int} \)  correspond to the integrated clustering coefficient, integrated characteristic path length, integrated normalized clustering coefficient, integrated normalized shortest path length, integrated global efficiency, and integrated local efficiency, respectively. doi:10.1371/journal.pone.0080214.t004
